title: Quick flips or long-term — sports card reseller interview prep (2020)
html:
Quick flips or long-term — sports card reseller interview prep (2020)

Source: 2020-09-23 Apple Note. Pandemic-era. Yahir prepping questions for a sports-cards reseller (likely interview / mentor convo). Same era as the rookie cards research and the 1997 Hoops pricing notes.

TL;DR

  • Strategic Q1: how does he scale — quick flips vs. long-term holds? — earliest version of the bridge income vs. compounding framing.
  • Grading mechanics questions: centering, quality, "can you grade if it's a bust?" — protocol-level due-diligence on the PSA grading system.
  • NBA names targeted: Tyler Herro, Devin Booker, Luka Doncic — rookie-era 2020 plays.
  • "Content in packs is gamble worth it?" / "Market, 90s overflooded, cycles?" — bubble-skepticism even while in the trade; same instinct that later distinguishes Rankett from a faddish AI tool.
  • Punchline question: "Is it a fad rn?" — Yahir asking the cycle-timing question at the apex of the COVID sports-card mania (2020-09 was peak).
  • "Reselling business, or asset business?" — terminology question that prefigures the value investing vs. flipping mental model.

Connections

  • bridge income strategies
  • value investing
  • surface area of luck
